title: Binary Search: The Optimal Strategy
content: The optimal number guessing strategy is binary search: always guess the midpoint of your current known range. If the answer is Too High, your new maximum is guess-1. If Too Low, your new minimum is guess+1. Each guess cuts the search space in half, minimizing total guesses needed.

title: Easy Difficulty Tips (Range 1-20)
content: Easy mode uses a range of 1 to 20 with 6 attempts. Binary search guarantees a win in at most 5 guesses (log2(20) ≈ 4.3). Start with 10. If Too High guess 5; if Too Low guess 15. Then keep halving the remaining range each turn.

title: Normal Difficulty Tips (Range 1-50)
content: Normal mode uses a range of 1 to 50 with 8 attempts. Binary search needs at most 6 guesses (log2(50) ≈ 5.6). Start with 25. Adjust by halving the remaining range after each Too High or Too Low feedback.

title: Hard Difficulty Tips (Range 1-100)
content: Hard mode uses a range of 1 to 100 with 5 attempts. Binary search needs at most 7 guesses (log2(100) ≈ 6.6), but you only have 5 attempts — so every guess must be the exact midpoint. Start with 50, then 25 or 75, then narrow precisely.

title: Responding to 'Too High' Feedback
content: When you receive Too High, the secret is strictly less than your guess. Update your upper bound: new_max = guess - 1. Your next guess should be the midpoint of (current_min, new_max). Never guess higher than your last Too High result.

title: Responding to 'Too Low' Feedback
content: When you receive Too Low, the secret is strictly greater than your guess. Update your lower bound: new_min = guess + 1. Your next guess should be the midpoint of (new_min, current_max). Never guess lower than your last Too Low result.

title: Managing Your Attempt Budget
content: Every wasted guess costs you — especially on Hard (5 attempts). Always guess the midpoint, never guess near the edges of your range unless forced. With 1 attempt left, pick the exact midpoint of your remaining range for the best probability of winning.

title: Maximizing Your Score
content: Score rewards efficiency: winning in 1 guess scores 80 points; in 2 guesses about 70; each extra guess reduces points by 10. Use binary search to win in as few guesses as possible. Avoid random guessing — it wastes your attempt budget and hurts your score.

title: How to Calculate the Midpoint
content: To find your next guess: midpoint = (lower_bound + upper_bound) // 2 (integer division). For example, range 1-50: guess 25. If Too High, range is 1-24: guess 12. If Too Low from 25, range is 26-50: guess 38. Always use integer division to stay within the valid range.

title: Best First Guesses by Difficulty
content: Start with the exact midpoint of your range for maximum information. Easy (1-20): start with 10. Normal (1-50): start with 25. Hard (1-100): start with 50. Starting with the midpoint gives you the most useful feedback regardless of what the secret number is.
